hits.title: Western Australia's longest straight stretch of road. Known as 90 ...
hits.summary:
Western Australia's longest straight stretch of road.
Known as 90 mile straight, it is 146.6km without a bend.
(91-mile) section of the Eyre Highway on the Nullarbor Plain in Western Australia, known as the "90 Mile Straight".
This iconic, featureless stretch of road, located between Balladonia and Caiguna, is a must-see on the Eyre Highway journey and is sometimes used as a landing strip by emergency services.

hits.title: The Long and Lonely Eyre Highway: Australia's Longest Straight Road
hits.summary:
With no hills or lakes to obstruct, the highway was laid down as a straight road that runs for 1,675 km from Port Augusta in the east to Norseman in the west, and includes what is said to be the longest straight stretch of road in ~~the world~~ Australia: 145.6 kilometres, between the small roadhouse communities of Balladonia and Caiguna (the longest straight road in the world is Highway 10 in Saudi Arabia, which is 162 miles long).
...
Between Balladonia and Caiguna, the road stretches for 145.6 kilometers without a bend.
This section of the highway, commonly known as the "90 Mile Straight", is regarded as the longest straight stretch of road in Australia and one of the longest in the world.

hits.summary:

To convert 10 kilometers to miles, you should follow the next steps:
1. Take the value in kilometers, in this case, the number 10.
2. Multiply it by **0.6214.**
3. Voila!
You have now converted the kilometers to miles; the result is **6.214.**
...
There are approximately **0.6214 miles in a kilometer.** To convert kilometers into miles, you need to multiply the length value in miles by the **conversion factor of 0.6214**.
